problem_statement: Expected: the nightly historical-archive package completes a mutually authenticated upload to the contracted preservation endpoint. Observed: every attempt stops during certificate validation before any package bytes are sent, although the archive application and payload are unchanged. Diagnose the causal family from captured local evidence only.

observations.statement: A bounded local log export records successful name resolution and TCP establishment, followed by certificate verify failed: expired intermediate; the upload counter remains zero bytes.

The incident's immutable peer-chain capture contains a preservation-provider intermediate whose notAfter precedes the incident by six hours; the leaf certificate, local client certificate, and two independent UTC readings are within validity.

Offline validation in an isolated process holds trust store, application revision, settings, runtime, and validation time fixed: the captured incident chain fails, while the provider chain captured the prior night validates.

Trust-store and application hashes match the previous successful night and a second archival endpoint validates under the same local process, despite a security-package update notice earlier in the week.
A previously captured provider incident notice for the same endpoint and time window reports incomplete certificate-chain rotation; no live status lookup was performed for this case.
